content: Self-Refine can be reframed as a stateless "lightweight self-reflection" baseline, while Reflexion can be understood as a memory-augmented stateful extension of Self-Refine that retains feedback from past iterations to refine future outputs more strategically. This complementary pairing reveals a spectrum of self-improving LLM systems, where researchers can trade off computational overhead and refinement performance by tuning how much past reflection memory is retained between iterations.
